story.iWant: the complete feature-based folder structure from the architecture spec
story.soThat: I know exactly where to place code for each epic and AI agents have clear boundaries
story.tasks: - Follow architecture.md#project-structure exactly - Create empty .gitkeep files in empty directories for git tracking - Add README.md in each feature folder explaining its purpose - Verify import paths work: import { Button } from '@/shared/components/ui/button'

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
Given the project has Tailwind configured (Story 1.2 complete) When I create the folder structure Then the following directories exist with index placeholder files: - src/features/assessment/ (components/, hooks/, types/, index.ts) - src/features/training/ (components/, drills/, hooks/, types/, index.ts) - src/features/coach/ (components/, hooks/, content/, index.ts) - src/features/cognition/ (components/, games/, index.ts) - src/features/progress/ (components/, hooks/, types/, index.ts) - src/features/magic-minute/ (components/, hooks/, index.ts) - src/shared/ (components/, components/ui/, hooks/, utils/, types/) - src/services/ (storage/, telemetry/, pwa/, adaptiveDifficulty/, research/) - src/context/ (AppContext.tsx, SessionContext.tsx, UserSettingsContext.tsx) - src/routes/ (Home.tsx, AssessmentRoute.tsx, TrainingRoute.tsx, ProgressRoute.tsx, ProfileRoute.tsx) - tests/e2e/ and tests/fixtures/
And Each index.ts exports a placeholder comment: // Public API for [feature-name] And src/shared/utils/constants.ts includes BREAKPOINTS constant from architecture And All folder names match architecture.md exactly (PascalCase for components, camelCase for services)
Prerequisites: Story 1.2 (Tailwind configured)

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Feature-based organization with src/features/ (assessment, training, coach, cognition, progress, magic-minute), src/shared/ (components, hooks, utils, types), src/services/ (storage, telemetry, pwa, adaptiveDifficulty, research), src/context/ (AppContext, SessionContext, UserSettingsContext), and src/routes/ (Home, AssessmentRoute, TrainingRoute, ProgressRoute, ProfileRoute).

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Each epic maps to specific feature folders: Assessment → features/assessment/, Training → features/training/, Coach → features/coach/, Cognition → features/cognition/, Progress → features/progress/, Magic Minute → features/magic-minute/, Research Mode → services/research/.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Story 1.3 delivers code organization and module boundaries. Inputs: Architecture specification. Outputs: Feature folders (assessment/, training/, etc.) and shared utilities. Establishes clear import paths for all subsequent stories.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Feature-based folder structure must match architecture.md exactly. All required directories exist: features/, shared/, services/, context/, routes/. Public API exports defined for each feature via index.ts files.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Test Strategy: Integration tests verify all directories exist via fs.existsSync(). Check each index.ts exports placeholder comment.

artifact.reason: Existing shadcn/ui components directory from Story 1.2. Should be moved/linked to src/shared/components/ui/ as per architecture spec.
Existing components directory. Architecture specifies src/shared/components/ instead. May need reorganization.
Existing styles directory from Story 1.2. Keep as-is per architecture (src/styles/).
Vite config includes @/ path alias. Verify it works with new folder structure: import from @/shared/components/ui/button should resolve correctly.

package.name: vite
package.note: Build tool with path alias support

story-context.constraints: - Folder names must match architecture.md exactly (case-sensitive) - PascalCase for component directories (Components, Routes) - camelCase for service directories (storage, telemetry, adaptiveDifficulty) - Each feature folder must have index.ts exporting public API - Placeholder comment format: // Public API for [feature-name] - Empty directories need .gitkeep files for git tracking - Each feature folder should have README.md explaining its purpose - @/ path alias must work: import { Button } from '@/shared/components/ui/button' - src/shared/components/ui/ must contain shadcn components (may need to move from src/components/ui/) - BREAKPOINTS constant required in src/shared/utils/constants.ts - 100% test coverage threshold enforced (Epic 1 requirement) - Prerequisites: Story 1.2 (Tailwind configured) must be completed first - All folder paths relative to project root - Maintain existing src/styles/ directory (not moved)

interface.name: Feature Module Public API
Shared Constants
Context Module Stubs
Route Component Stubs

tests.standards: Epic 1 establishes 100% test coverage requirement using Vitest + React Testing Library. Folder structure verification is integration-level testing using Node.js fs module. AAA pattern (Arrange-Act-Assert) mandatory. Tests must verify: 1) All required directories exist, 2) Each index.ts contains placeholder comment, 3) Path alias (@/) resolves correctly, 4) BREAKPOINTS constant is defined.
tests.locations: - Integration test: src/__tests__/folder-structure.test.ts (verify directory existence) - Integration test: src/__tests__/module-exports.test.ts (verify index.ts placeholders) - Integration test: src/__tests__/path-alias.test.ts (verify @/ imports work) - Unit test: src/shared/utils/constants.test.ts (verify BREAKPOINTS constant)
tests.ideas: - AC (Feature folders): Use Node.js fs.existsSync() to verify src/features/assessment/, src/features/training/, src/features/coach/, src/features/cognition/, src/features/progress/, src/features/magic-minute/ all exist - AC (Shared folders): Verify src/shared/components/, src/shared/components/ui/, src/shared/hooks/, src/shared/utils/, src/shared/types/ exist - AC (Services folders): Verify src/services/storage/, src/services/telemetry/, src/services/pwa/, src/services/adaptiveDifficulty/, src/services/research/ exist - AC (Context files): Verify src/context/AppContext.tsx, src/context/SessionContext.tsx, src/context/UserSettingsContext.tsx exist - AC (Route files): Verify src/routes/Home.tsx, src/routes/AssessmentRoute.tsx, src/routes/TrainingRoute.tsx, src/routes/ProgressRoute.tsx, src/routes/ProfileRoute.tsx exist - AC (Test folders): Verify tests/e2e/ and tests/fixtures/ exist - AC (Index placeholders): Read each src/features/*/index.ts and assert contains "// Public API for" comment - AC (Constants): Import BREAKPOINTS from '@/shared/utils/constants' and verify equals { mobile: 320, tablet: 768, desktop: 1024 } - AC (Path alias): Test import statement "import { Button } from '@/shared/components/ui/button'" compiles without TypeScript errors - AC (Naming): Verify all folder names match architecture.md (case-sensitive check)
